title: Colymbada × tatayana
taxonomy: Colymbada × tatayana is a hybrid species of plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family, Asteraceae. Colymbada × tatayana is derived from the genera Colymbada and Tanacetum. It is known to be a hybrid of Colymbada and Tanacetum. The classification of this plant is primarily based on its botanical characteristics and genetic makeup. Its classification is subject to ongoing research and study in the field of botany.

intro: Colymbada × tatayana, also known as the hybrid of Colymbada (formerly Senecio) and a wild species of the genus Colymbada, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to various health benefits such as improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function. The bioactive constituents of Colymbada × tatayana, particularly sesquiterpene lactones, have been shown to exhibit potential anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory effects. Colymbada × tatayana is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into supplements, to support overall health and well-being.
